notes: KNOWN FAILING (daemon gap, issue filed by dispatcher) — the long-idle-live-session premise (a session stays alive past readyTTL via PID-liveness) does NOT hold for antigravity. agy never binds a PID to its conv-id session (it reports its sandbox scratch dir, not the launch cwd, as the run_command Cwd, so DiscoverPID can't match the agy process), so the session is pid=0. The daemon's readyTTL sweep reaps a pid=0 session past the TTL even when its transcript file is fresh and the agy process is alive (core/application/services/pid_manager.go; documented in pid_manager_test.go:118 'a pid=0 session whose UpdatedAt is past readyTTL is reaped... even with a fresh transcript on disk, since the daemon can't probe liveness of a pid=0 session'). LIVE EVIDENCE (recording 2026-06-20, IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s): session born ready 12:39:01 -> working 12:39:03 -> ready 12:39:07, then transcript_removed 12:39:27 (~20s idle, past the 15s TTL) DURING the idle window, before the second prompt; the second prompt's transcript_activity at 12:39:29 produced NO state_transition because the session row was already gone. For a transcript-first adapter a fresh transcript IS the liveness signal, so reaping it is the gap. The recording captures the reliable arc up to the reap; the idle_window 'no transcript_removed' invariant FAILS by design until the daemon stops reaping pid=0 sessions with a fresh transcript (or an alive ExactName process). Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL); model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> reports 'Gemini 3.1 Pro'.

text: Conv-id session appears in ready after the first turn
First user prompt drives ready -> working
First turn completes back to ready
KNOWN FAILING: spec asserts the session does NOT disappear past readyTTL; antigravity's pid=0 session is reaped (transcript_removed) during the idle window — the daemon gap this cell documents.
